steps.title: Make two data rows
steps.content: The value is $150$ when $x=0$. Each increase of $1$ in $x$ multiplies $y$ by $4$, so the next point is $(1,600)$. These two points are enough to fit the exponential model.

steps.title: Use exponential regression
steps.content: In Desmos, enter $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. The regression gives $a=150$ and $b=4$, so the model is $y=150(4)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer choice
steps.content: Choice A shifts the exponent and gives $37.5$ when $x=0$, not $150$. Choice C and choice D use $150$ as the base, so the multiplier is wrong. Choice B, $y=150(4)^x$, has initial value $150$ and multiplier $4$. Choice B is correct.
